rationale: In the citing paper, Berger and Schwartz (2011) is used mainly in grouped citations in the General Discussion and future-research sections to support broad points about social transmission and accessibility cues. The article does not rely on that paper to build its core hypotheses, does not adopt its immediate-versus-ongoing WOM framework, and does not reuse its methods or measures. This indicates a background corroborative role rather than a distinct, central dependency.
